title: baccharis pilularis subsp. pilularis
intro: Baccharis pilularis subsp. pilularis, also known as the coast coyote brush or scrub baccharis, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its high content of volatile oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Baccharis pilularis subsp. pilularis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, often used by indigenous communities in traditional medicine for its soothing and protective effects.
